surface.name: Camunda 8 local Rocket Launch first BPMN process
surface.selection_basis: The official Get started page tells new users to run their first local Camunda 8 project and presents Run your first BPMN process first. That beginner guide provides a self-contained, no-code Rocket Launch project, exact local startup and deployment actions, sample variables, and an explicit completed-process verification in Operate.

alternatives_considered.name: First Spring Boot or Node.js project
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This route is for developers implementing service workers and adds a language and framework choice after the general no-code BPMN starting route.

alternatives_considered.name: Build your first AI agent
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The AI-agent guide is a specialized second card rather than the first general getting-started project.

alternatives_considered.name: Camunda 8 SaaS
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The getting-started page explicitly starts with a local project, avoiding a hosted account and organization path.

entry_point.developer_intent: Start without a Camunda account or local installation, download the official package, start Camunda 8 locally, deploy and run the supplied Rocket Launch BPMN model with the documented successful input, and verify the completed instance and result variables in Operate.
assumed_prior_state: No Camunda account, Camunda software, local engine, deployed model, process instance, or custom code is assumed.
A supported Mac, Linux, or Windows computer, browser, file-unzip capability, terminal, JDK 21 through 25, network connection, and local execution permission are available.
No package was downloaded, local service was started, model was deployed, or process instance was executed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Run your first BPMN process with Camunda 8.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The supplied Rocket Launch BPMN process runs locally to successful completion with the documented `Odyssey` mission and `fuelLevel` of 95.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Operate shows the process instance completed successfully, and its Variables panel includes the generated destination, remaining fuel, and mission result; with fuel level 95, the documented logic selects Venus and five experiments.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The guide is titled Run your first BPMN process with Camunda 8, instructs the developer to verify completed instances, and begins Next steps with 'Now that you've run your first BPMN process in Camunda 8'.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: A completed local instance with visible result variables is the first functioning orchestration outcome. Trying additional fuel scenarios, understanding model internals, stopping the local environment, service-worker projects, agents, human tasks, and connector work are optional or later activities.

prerequisites.requirement: Install JDK 21 through 25, which Camunda 8 Run requires to start the local engine.
Be able to download and unzip the Camunda 8 Getting Started Package and run its operating-system-specific startup script.
Have a browser and local access to `http://localhost:8080/operate`.

primary_path.action: Open Get started with Camunda and select Run your first BPMN process.
details: The page frames this as the first local Camunda 8 project.
The project is fully automated and requires no code.
primary_path.output: The Rocket Launch beginner guide is open.
primary_path.success_signal: The guide describes a local BPMN process that performs pre-flight, decision, destination, and mission-report steps.

primary_path.action: Confirm that JDK 21 through 25 is installed, then download the Getting Started Package from Camunda Downloads.
details: The package contains Camunda 8 Run, Camunda Modeler, and the getting-started projects including Rocket Launch.
primary_path.input: A supported computer with the required JDK
primary_path.output: The official package archive is available locally.
primary_path.success_signal: The archive contains the local runtime, Modeler, and example-project files named by the guide.

primary_path.action: Unzip the package, change to its directory, and start Camunda 8 Run with the command for the operating system.
primary_path.success_signal: The packaged local services become available for Modeler and Operate.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The guide does not document a startup failure or recovery sequence.

primary_path.action: Open Camunda Modeler, select File and Open File, and open `camunda-8-get-started/1-rocket-launch/rocket-launch.bpmn`.
details: The model and its related DMN resource are supplied in the package.
primary_path.output: The Rocket Launch BPMN model is displayed in Modeler.
primary_path.success_signal: The model is open with the deployment and play controls available.

primary_path.action: Click the rocket icon, retain the preconfigured `c8run (local)` connection, and click Deploy.
details: Deploy sends all project resources, including the DMN decision table, to the local engine.
primary_path.output: The BPMN model and DMN table are deployed locally.
primary_path.success_signal: Modeler completes the deployment through the local connection.

primary_path.action: Click Play and start a process instance with `missionName` set to `Odyssey` and `fuelLevel` set to 95.
details: The guide publishes this JSON object as its example first-instance input.
primary_path.output: A Rocket Launch process instance begins.
primary_path.success_signal: The local engine accepts the instance and executes the supplied process.

primary_path.action: Open `http://localhost:8080/operate`, sign in with `demo` and `demo`, find Rocket Launch, and open the running instance.
details: The process may not appear immediately because data must sync to Operate.
primary_path.output: The process instance is visible in Operate.
primary_path.success_signal: The Rocket Launch instance page opens.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wait for Operate data synchronization if the instance is not yet visible.

primary_path.action: Execute the process through its Countdown T-10 timer and mission logic.
details: The instance pauses at Countdown T-10 for 10 seconds.
Fuel level 95 takes the successful branch and selects Venus with five experiments.
primary_path.output: The process reaches its successful end event.
primary_path.success_signal: Operate marks the instance completed successfully.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The documented countdown adds a 10-second pause.

primary_path.action: Open the completed instance's Variables panel and inspect the generated result variables.
details: The guide names `destination`, `fuelAfterBurn`, and `missionResult` as generated variables.
Its mission-report expression records the mission name, destination, remaining fuel, and experiment count.
primary_path.output: The generated mission outcome is visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The completed successful instance and its Venus mission result establish the first BPMN process run.

branches.condition: The developer uses Mac or Linux
branches.path: Start with `./camunda-start.sh` and, after the success boundary if desired, stop with `./camunda-stop.sh`.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Uses the Unix script names published by the guide.

branches.path: Start with `.\camunda-start.bat` and, after the success boundary if desired, stop with `.\camunda-stop.bat`.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Uses the Windows script names published by the guide.

branches.path: The launch succeeds, selects Venus, and runs five experiments.
branches.effect_on_first_success: This is the successful branch taken by the guide's example value of 95.

branches.condition: Fuel level is 76 through 89, exactly 75, or 50 through 74
branches.path: The launch succeeds with the documented Mars or Moon destination and three or five experiments according to the value.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The process completes through a different successful mission path.

branches.path: The process takes the NO-GO cancellation path and ends as a canceled mission.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The instance reaches a documented terminal, but not the successful launch used for the primary route.

friction_gates.description: The local runtime requires JDK 21 through 25.
The developer must download and unzip the Getting Started Package containing the runtime, Modeler, and example.
Startup and optional shutdown commands differ between Windows and Mac or Linux.
A local Camunda runtime must remain running for Modeler deployment and Operate inspection.
The local Operate interface requires the documented `demo` and `demo` credentials.
The instance may not appear in Operate immediately because its data must synchronize.
The example process deliberately pauses for 10 seconds at Countdown T-10.

sources.title: Run your first BPMN process with Camunda 8 | Camunda 8 Docs
sections_used: Step 1: Download the Getting Started Package
Step 2: Deploy and run your model
Step 3: Explore your process in Operate

sources.title: Get started with Camunda | Camunda 8 Docs
sections_used: Run your first BPMN process with Camunda 8
Run your first Spring Boot or Node.js project with Camunda 8

excluded_after_success: Running additional fuel scenarios, comparing canceled instances, studying the FEEL and DMN implementation, stopping the local runtime, adding service workers, building an AI agent, orchestrating human tasks, and using connectors occur after the first successful completed process is visible.
